title: Cyclosorus evolutus
intro: Cyclosorus evolutus, also known as the evolute leather fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Blechnaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus evolutus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of dried leaves or capsules for its medicinal benefits.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus evolutus is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which belongs to the order Polypodiales, and the class Polypodiopsida. It is a species of fern that is commonly found in tropical regions. The classification of Cyclosorus evolutus is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the division Pteridophyta. This fern species is characterized by its distinctive leaf morphology and habitat requirements. As a member of the Thelypteridaceae family, Cyclosorus evolutus is closely related to other species of ferns within this family.
